intro: Lycopodioides kirkii, also known as New Zealand club moss, is a species of vascular plant belonging to the family Lycopodiaceae, characterized by its small, scale-like leaves and red or brown sporophylls. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can aid in wound healing and the treatment of infections.
